chapter_title.ar: باب مَنْ يَدْخُلُ الْقَبْرَ وَ مَنْ لَا يَدْخُلُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 63 – The one who enters the grave and the one who does not enter

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/90)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/90)
text: هُ سَأَلَ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الْقَبْرِ كَمْ يَدْخُلُهُ قَالَ ذَاكَ إِلَى الْوَلِيِّ إِنْ شَاءَ أَدْخَلَ وَتْراً وَ إِنْ شَاءَ شَفْعاً .

en.hubeali: Abu Ali Al Ashary, from Muhammad Bin Abdul Jabbar, from Abdullah Al Hajjal, from Sa’alba Bin Maymoun,

from Zurara who asked Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> about the grave, how many can enter into it. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘That is up to the guardian (of the deceased). If he so desires to he can (allow) an odd number, and if he so desires to he can (allow) an even number (of people)’.
en.sarwar: 4. Abu Ali al-Ash’ariy has narrated from Muhammad ibn ‘Abd al-Jabbar from ‘Abd Allah al-Hajjal from Tha’labah ibn Maymun from Zurarah who has said the following: “I asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, ‘How many should enter a grave?’ He replied, ‘It is up to the guardian of the deceased. He may ask an odd or even number of people.’”
